subject: Product Management
title: The Power of Defaults
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Power of Defaults.

hook.text: Google paid Apple $15 billion in one year to be the default search engine on Safari. Defaults are worth billions.

buildup.text: Most users never change default settings. Studies show 90% of people accept whatever option is pre-selected.

discovery.text: Microsoft won the browser war not with a better product, but by making Internet Explorer the Windows default.

twist.text: Defaults aren't manipulation — they're product decisions. Choosing good defaults is designing for how people actually behave.

climax.text: Every default setting is a product decision with massive consequences. The default is the product for 90% of users.

punchline.text: Pick the right defaults. Most users will never change them.

quiz.question: Why are default settings so important in product management?
options: 90% of users never change defaults — the default IS the product for most people
Defaults have no impact on user behavior
Defaults only matter for enterprise software
